routes.rope_note: Class 3 scramble; no route-specific trip reports found describing roped sections. Typical for this grade in the Olympics/Cascades to go unroped.
routes.corrections: No route-specific gear source found; based on Class 3 scramble grade convention.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 scramble on scree/talus; standard scramble kit, no rope typically carried.
routes.corrections: Route-specific detail limited; South Slopes conflated with general Mt Seattle east-ridge trip notes in search results.

routes.rope_note: ~3,000 ft of 40-50 deg snow/glacial ice to gain the upper Hanging Glacier, then 4th-5th class rock to the summit pyramid; used as an AMGA guide exam route for its sustained, moderate difficulty.

sling_rack.cams: light rack to 2in for summit pyramid rock

routes.corrections: Distinguished from the standard North Face route it shares an approach with; no dedicated Hanging-Glacier-specific trip report with an itemized rack was found, so gear is extrapolated from North Face route profile.

routes.rope_note: Described as a seldom-done line combining additional ice climbing with complex alpine rock climbing above the North Face; more sustained/technical than the standard glacier routes.
routes.corrections: Very little documented beta exists for this specific line (confirmed rare/seldom-climbed); gear inferred from its stated character (ice + complex rock) and Shuksan's other technical routes.

routes.rope_note: Fifty Classic Climbs route; AI2-3 lower glacier ice, bergschrund crux around 7,800 ft with rock/ice/mixed options. A documented mid-August ascent used nearly all pro carried.
routes.corrections: None — multiple independent trip reports converge on this gear list.

routes.rope_type: single 30m glacier rope (or 60m if combining with Winnies Slide/Fisher Chimneys exit)
routes.rope_note: Complex glacier route with short 1-2 pitch snow/rock/ice sections crossing bergschrunds; joins Winnies Slide about 2/3 height. Requires solid belaying and moderate ice technique.
routes.corrections: Grade II/easy-snow characterization from the route list is a bit generous — sources describe short technical ice/rock steps requiring real belaying skill, not a walk-up.

routes.rope_note: Only the final ~600 ft above the notch is technical; class 3 approach pitches often simul-hiked or unroped. Selective route-finding keeps hardest moves at 5.3-5.4.

sling_rack.slings: extra slings for horn anchors, common on this route

routes.corrections: None — matches Mountain Project route description directly.

routes.rope_note: Typical Class 2-3(3+) Olympic scramble: side-hilling, contouring, talus, rotten rock via Putvin Trail/Lake of the Angels. Steep snow lingers in the approach gully/headwall into early summer.
routes.corrections: Ice axe need for early-season steep snow is inferred from the grade note, not from an explicit trip-report gear list.

routes.rope_note: Long approach (21 mi via Duckabush to Marmot Lake) then easy slopes to the summit block; wet conditions common June-early July.
routes.corrections: Route-specific gear list not found (only approach/access descriptions); technical difficulty and gear inferred from Class 2-3 grade.

routes.rope_type: none typically; short rope/handline used in winter
routes.rope_note: Class 3-4 talus/dirt scramble to a summit ridge/plateau; most parties go unroped in summer. In winter most groups rope up for the final gully to the summit ridge.
routes.corrections: None — matches route-specific description including the winter rope-up note.

routes.rope_note: Short Class 3 section on Mt Stone's west ridge with three ~20 ft downclimbs; some parties want a handline for these. Loose glacial moraine near St. Peter's Gate has a permanent snow/ice field — ice axe recommended.
routes.corrections: None — matches Mountain Project route page and multiple trip reports.

routes.rope_note: Predominantly Class 3 with brief Class 4 moves on the summit block; ~5,000 ft gain, 11 mi RT via Putvin Trail. Typically climbed unroped by scramblers comfortable with exposure.
routes.corrections: None — consistent across sources.

routes.rope_note: Same approach/line as the South Route via Lake of the Angels (Putvin Trail IS the Lake of the Angels approach); Class 3 with brief Class 4 summit block moves.
routes.corrections: This route_id appears to describe the same physical line as wa_mount_stone_lake_of_angels — flagging as likely duplicate/alias in the DB rather than a distinct line.

routes.rope_note: 8 pitches, ~900 ft, splitter crack on the West Face between Goat and Stuart Pass; P2/P4/P6 run 55-60m so a 60m rope is workable but tight on the longest pitches.

sling_rack.cams: single blue alien/purple TCU; doubles green alien to #2; single #3 and #4

routes.corrections: None — pulled directly from the route's Mountain Project page.

routes.rope_note: Climbs first 4 pitches of Gorillas in the Mist, cuts into 2 pitches of Gorillas Direct, then 2 pitches of direct splitter headwall finish. Three pitches run ~55m, making 70m more comfortable than 60m.

sling_rack.cams: base rack as Gorillas Direct plus supplemental 0.5-#4 recommended for headwall security

routes.corrections: Route list grade given as 5.11d matches AAC/Supertopo sourcing.

routes.rope_note: Glacier approach/couloir route reaching ~40 deg near the top; ice axe and crampons essential as upper glacier and beyond can be firm/icy.
routes.corrections: None — consistent across sources.

routes.rope_note: 4-5 short technical pitches (mostly 4th/low-5th) with two 20-30 ft sections of 5.7 that have decent pro; approach via Ingalls Creek Trail/West Ridge couloir.
routes.corrections: No itemized rack list found specific to this route; sizes inferred from the described 5.7 crack/corner terrain typical of Stuart Range alpine rock.

routes.rope_note: Striking pitch-3 liebeck crack, Great Gendarme offwidth crux, 5.5 slab with a good crack up top. Rock 'loose in spots, lichen covered' in places.

sling_rack.cams: 0.3-3, doubles 0.4-2, optional #4 for the Gendarme offwidth

routes.corrections: None — pulled directly from the route's Mountain Project page.

routes.rope_note: One of the Fifty Classic Climbs. ~11 pitches of low/mid 5th from the Stuart Glacier notch to the Gendarme base, then two crux 5.9 pitches plus a 5.8-5.9 pitch before 3 final low-5th pitches. A fixed #4 Camalot has been reported in place on the offwidth.

sling_rack.cams: medium alpine rack to 4in, doubles 0.5-3in; bring one 4in piece for the offwidth

routes.corrections: None — consistent across multiple sources.

routes.rope_note: Approach via Degenhardt Glacier requiring ice axe/crampons. Lower face ascending traverse mostly 3rd class stepping to 5.7 near the buttress crest; upper face has a long offwidth crack below the false summit.

sling_rack.cams: medium alpine rack, emphasis on medium sizes for the offwidth section

routes.corrections: None — consistent across sources.

routes.rope_note: Ascends slabs to the Terror-Degenhardt saddle then a short steep ridge with a hand-width crack start; rock quality is notably poor/loose per trip reports.
routes.corrections: Route list names this 'East Ridge' under the wa_mount_terror_southeast_face id — flagging the id/name mismatch for DB review.

routes.rope_type: single 30-60m dynamic (carried mainly for the descent)
routes.rope_note: Mostly Class 2 via Bumblebee Pass approach with one exposed ~20 ft Class 4 move near the summit; many parties rope up for this move.
routes.corrections: None — consistent across sources.
